From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

College basketball team

| LIU Brooklyn Blackbirds | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| University | [Long Island University](/wiki/The_Brooklyn_Campus_of_Long_Island_University "The Brooklyn Campus of Long Island University") |
| First season | 1965 (folded in 2019; 7 years ago (2019)) |
| Location | [Brooklyn, New York](/wiki/Brooklyn,_New_York "Brooklyn, New York") |
| Arena | [Steinberg Wellness Center](/wiki/Steinberg_Wellness_Center "Steinberg Wellness Center")  (capacity: 3,000) |
| [Conference](/wiki/Athletic_conference#Collegiate_sports "Athletic conference") | [Northeast Conference](/wiki/Northeast_Conference "Northeast Conference") |
| Nickname | [Blackbirds](/wiki/LIU_Brooklyn_Blackbirds "LIU Brooklyn Blackbirds") |
| Colors | Black and silver[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| NCAA Division I tournament appearances | |
| **2001** | |

The **LIU Brooklyn Blackbirds women's basketball** team represented the [Brooklyn campus](/wiki/LIU_Brooklyn "LIU Brooklyn") of [Long Island University](/wiki/Long_Island_University "Long Island University"), located in [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn"), [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)") in [NCAA](/wiki/National_Collegiate_Athletic_Association "National Collegiate Athletic Association") [Division I](/wiki/NCAA_Division_I "NCAA Division I") [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") competition. They played their home games at the [Steinberg Wellness Center](/wiki/Steinberg_Wellness_Center "Steinberg Wellness Center"), formerly known as the Wellness, Recreation & Athletics Center, and were members of the [Northeast Conference](/wiki/Northeast_Conference "Northeast Conference") (NEC).[[2]](#cite_note-2)

At the end of the 2018–19 school year, LIU merged its two athletic programs—the Division I program of the Brooklyn campus and the [NCAA Division II](/wiki/NCAA_Division_II "NCAA Division II") [program](/wiki/LIU_Post_Pioneers "LIU Post Pioneers") of its [Post campus](/wiki/LIU_Post "LIU Post") in [Nassau County, New York](/wiki/Nassau_County,_New_York "Nassau County, New York")—into a single Division I program that now competes as the [LIU Sharks](/wiki/LIU_Sharks_women%27s_basketball "LIU Sharks women's basketball").[[3]](#cite_note-3) The Sharks maintain Brooklyn's Division I and NEC memberships.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Brooklyn_Blackbirds_women%27s_basketball&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The Blackbirds began play in 1965.[[4]](#cite_note-4) They have made one NCAA Tournament appearance (2001) and one WNIT appearance (2007), losing in the First Round each time, 101-29 to Connecticut and 91-79 to Iona, respectively.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## NCAA tournament results

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Brooklyn_Blackbirds_women%27s_basketball&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: NCAA tournament results")]

| Year | Seed | Round | Opponent | Result |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [2001](/wiki/2001_NCAA_Division_I_women%27s_basketball_tournament "2001 NCAA Division I women's basketball tournament") | #16 | First Round | #1 Connecticut | L 29-101 |
